meaning: su:pEkRndQk5tIv/adj.[物]超传tIvItI/n.[物]超档枷窒蟮,超电导的/嫉缧
examples: Experimental studies were made in Josephson effect of bulk superconductive materials at liquid nitrogen temperature.
The superconductive technology of the high temperature is more and more widely used because of its extensive superiority.
At extremely low temperatures, some metals and alloys lose their resistance to electrical current and become superconductive.
